name: database-migrations-sql-migrations
description: SQL database migrations with zero-downtime strategies for
qualityReason: Focused on critical SQL migration safety
capabilities: Generate expand-contract migration patterns for zero-downtime schema changes
Create blue-green deployment strategies with dual-write synchronization
Write validation scripts for pre and post-migration data integrity checks
Build automated rollback procedures with database snapshots
Optimize large table migrations using batch processing and parallel execution
Manage indexes during bulk operations with concurrent index creation
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 63 static findings are false positives. The skill provides legitimate SQL migration examples including PostgreSQL functions (gen_random_uuid, pg_sleep), database introspection queries (pg_indexes), and configuration references. No actual command injection, weak cryptography, or data exfiltration present. This is a benign database migration guidance skill.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers implement zero-downtime database migrations for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server with built-in rollback procedures and validation checks.
limitations: Does not execute migrations - provides guidance and scripts only
Does not support NoSQL databases like MongoDB or DynamoDB
Requires manual review of generated SQL before production execution
Does not provide built-in CI/CD integration

useCases.title: Add new column without downtime
useCases.description: Safely add a NOT NULL column to a large production table using the expand-contract pattern with batched data backfill.

useCases.title: Migrate legacy schema to new structure
useCases.description: Use blue-green migration with dual-write synchronization to migrate from legacy to new schema with zero data loss.

useCases.title: Create safe rollback procedures
useCases.description: Generate automated rollback scripts with pre-migration snapshots and validation checks to recover from failed migrations.

promptTemplates.title: Basic migration request
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a migration script to add a new column called email_verified (BOOLEAN, DEFAULT FALSE) to the users table in PostgreSQL. Include proper error handling.

promptTemplates.title: Zero-downtime migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a zero-downtime migration strategy to convert a VARCHAR column to ENUM type in a production MySQL database with 10M+ rows.

promptTemplates.title: Data migration with validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a migration to move data from the legacy orders table to a new v2_orders table. Include pre-validation checks, batched backfill, and post-migration row count verification.

promptTemplates.title: Complete migration with rollback
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a full migration package for adding a JSONB metadata column to the products table. Include: forward migration SQL, rollback SQL, validation checks, and a shell script for execution with proper error handling.

bestPractices: Always create a database snapshot or backup before running migrations in production
Use transaction blocks (BEGIN/COMMIT) to ensure atomicity of migration steps
Validate row counts before and after migration to detect data loss
Test migrations on a staging environment that mirrors production
antiPatterns: Running migrations directly on production without pre-migration validation
Using ALTER TABLE without CONCURRENTLY flag for large tables in PostgreSQL
Skipping rollback procedures for schema changes in production
Not considering foreign key constraints and referential integrity during migrations

faq.question: What databases does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server. For NoSQL databases like MongoDB, use the nosql-migrations skill.

faq.question: Does this skill execute migrations?
faq.answer: No, this skill generates migration scripts and guidance. You must review and execute the generated SQL yourself in your database environment.

faq.question: How do I ensure zero-downtime during migrations?
faq.answer: Use expand-contract patterns: add new columns/tables first (backward compatible), migrate data in batches, then remove old structures after code deployment.

faq.question: Can I use this with Flyway or Alembic?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes examples for both Flyway and Alembic migration frameworks. The generated scripts can be adapted to your existing migration tooling.

faq.question: What validation checks should I run?
faq.answer: Run pre-migration checks (NULL values, duplicates, foreign key integrity) and post-migration checks (row counts, constraint validation, index existence).

faq.question: How do I handle failed migrations?
faq.answer: Use savepoints within transactions, create pre-migration backups, and have rollback scripts ready. The skill provides detailed rollback procedure examples.
